title: Native leader and land claims pioneer passes away at 84
summary: Don Wright with President Richard Nixon. Courtesy of Doyon Ltd. A driving force behind the passage of the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act (ANCSA) and former president of the Alaska Federation of Natives (AFN), CIRI shareholder Don Wright passed away peacefully in his home in Kenai on July 5. He was 84. Wright was born in Nenana in 1929. His father...

title: Village of Tyonek unites to fight and recover from recent wildfire
summary: On May 27, when a much-needed rain descended upon Southcentral Alaska, residents of Tyonek saw the beginning of an end to a wildfire that had blazed for almost 10 days. The fire burned through 1,906 acres, causing a temporary evacuation of the village and displacing more than 100 residents for sever...

title: A word from the president, July 2014
summary: By Sophie Minich, CIRI president and chief executive officer Every year, federal, state and local governments make decisions or pass laws that affect Alaska Native people. Subsistence rights, educational funding, development of Native-owned land – so many aspects of our lives are impacted by decisions made at one level of government or another. It’s easy to get frustrated with lawmakers when the regulations they put in place don’t reflect o...
